subject: Change PAP Urls called
commit_message:
Change PAP Urls called

Due to the changes done on the PAP Url (+return code), the camel flows must be adapted. There is no change required for the return code PAP change as Camel already catches all non 2XX code.

Issue-ID: POLICY-3068
Signed-off-by: sebdet <sebastien.determe@intl.att.com>
Change-Id: I77549f944d28acd5e39e4d9e7e47688146db3681

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Note: the old "deployed" API gave back a single record per policy, while the new "status" API gives back a record per policy/PDP pair.  In light of this, are any additional changes needed?

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Agree with Jim's comment. The response payload has changed in old "deployed" vs new "status" API. In case you were parsing the payload, there will be changes required.

Also, to know the actual status of the policy, you will have to parse the response payload and check the "state" field. As the API response will always be 200 whether the "state" is waiting, success or failure.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
> 
> Note: the old "deployed" API gave back a single record per policy, while the new "status" API gives back a record per policy/PDP pair.  In light of this, are any additional changes needed?

Currently we do not check the payload, that's why there is no impact, we check only the return code. It used to work because when it was undeployed due to errors the return code was not 2XX.

That's what i was trying to understand during our call, will the behavior payload/return code change or not 😊

>

To try to clarify:
- the APIs to DO the deployment (e.g., simple deploy, group deploy, batch deploy) will now return 202 instead of 200; there is no change to the structure of the response
- the existing API to query deployment status is unchanged, though it will likely be deprecated during Istanbul
- we are introducing a new API to query deployment status.  This API returns individual records for each Policy/PDP pair

Yes Jim, thx for clarification, as we want to move to the new deployment status api, what is missing here is the analysis of the response as the http code will always be 200.
I'm going to change that

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

As discussed with Seb,clamp is using the api with filters - /policy/pap/v1/policies/status/{pdpGroupName}/{policyName}/{policyVersion}

For deployment,
Initially it will return 200, with “state” in payload as WAITING. This is just an interim state when PAP is waiting for PDPs to confirm back.

If it succeeds then it will return 200, with “state” in payload as SUCCESS. This will remain as long as policy is not undeployed.

If it fails then it will return 404. And you continue to get 404 until the policy is deployed again.

However, there is a chance of getting 200 with “state” in payload as FAILURE. But this changes so quickly to 404. So we haven’t seen it yet during our testing.

For undeployment,
Initially it will return 200, with “state” in payload as WAITING. This is just an interim state when PAP is waiting for PDPs to confirm back.

If it succeeds then it will return 404. And you continue to get 404 until the policy is deployed again.

If it fails then it will return 404. And you continue to get 404 until the policy is deployed again.

@Ajith & @Jim - Please verify my understanding.

Patch Set 1:

So after having discussed that with Ram, there is no need to change anything here. The response should not be verified as clamp is using that API /policy/pap/v1/policies/status/{pdpGroupName}/{policyName}/{policyVersion}

According to Ram, in case of failure to deploy:
"There is a chance of getting 200 with “state” in payload as FAILURE. But this changes so quickly to 404. So we haven’t seen it yet." Ram Said

So the behavior is quite similar to the previous/deprecated api "/deployed", it return 200 with "WAITING", then switches to SUCCESS. In that case it always return 200 so clamp knows that everything is ok. In case of failure it will finally return 404 so clamp will detect the problem and reports it properly.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> So after having discussed that with Ram, there is no need to change anything here. The response should not be verified as clamp is using that API /policy/pap/v1/policies/status/{pdpGroupName}/{policyName}/{policyVersion}
> 
> According to Ram, in case of failure to deploy:
> "There is a chance of getting 200 with “state” in payload as FAILURE. But this changes so quickly to 404. So we haven’t seen it yet." Ram Said
> 
> So the behavior is quite similar to the previous/deprecated api "/deployed", it return 200 with "WAITING", then switches to SUCCESS. In that case it always return 200 so clamp knows that everything is ok. In case of failure it will finally return 404 so clamp will detect the problem and reports it properly.

Right.  The primary difference is that the response may contain multiple records, one for each PDP, some of which could be SUCCESS, some WAITING, and some FAILURE (though as Ram said, FAILURE only lasts for a brief instant before it undeploys the policy - in fact, I'm not sure the response will ever contain a FAILURE for a deployment).
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully merged by Gervais-Martial Ngueko

We probably need to revisit that when the policy will be deployed in multiple PDP groups, but creently this is not the case at all.

Patch Set 1:

> We probably need to revisit that when the policy will be deployed in multiple PDP groups, but creently this is not the case at all.

Actually, the issue can arise if there is more than one of a given type of PDP in the same group, which is already a possibility.

Jim, not sure to see the case you are talking about, here we use this api to get the deployment status: /policy/pap/v1/policies/status/{pdpGroupName}/{policyName}/{policyVersion}
We specify the pdpGroupName, policyName and version so how can it return multiple entries ?
